name: API design
when_to_use: Designing a new HTTP/RPC API or major version

name: SDK design
when_to_use: Designing a new client library or binding

name: CLI design
when_to_use: Designing a new CLI or major subcommand

name: Docs design
when_to_use: Designing the developer docs for a new surface — quickstart, reference, tutorials, troubleshooting

name: Error design
when_to_use: Designing the error response shape and recovery copy

name: Setup design
when_to_use: Designing install path and first-run experience

name: Inner-loop design
when_to_use: Designing the local dev experience for a new project

name: Contributor design
when_to_use: Designing repo onboarding, PR template, review expectations

name: Auth design
when_to_use: Designing token UX, scopes, rotation, error states

name: Migration design
when_to_use: Designing an upgrade path, codemod, or deprecation lifecycle

name: Plugin design
when_to_use: Designing a plugin API or extension-point contract

name: IDE-integration design
when_to_use: Designing autocomplete/LSP/hover-doc behavior

name: Perf design
when_to_use: Designing install/startup/build-time budgets and instrumentation

name: Telemetry design
when_to_use: Designing data collection, consent, and opt-out

name: README design
when_to_use: Designing the README and first-impressions surface for a new project

name: Package design
when_to_use: Designing publish, semver discipline, peer deps, install footprint, types shipped

name: Examples design
when_to_use: Designing the samples surface, per-language hello-world strategy, CI for examples

name: Logging design
when_to_use: Designing log levels, verbosity dial, structured output, doctor/status/version commands

name: Config design
when_to_use: Designing runtime config: file shape, env-var precedence, validation, dump-effective-config

name: Changelog design
when_to_use: Designing the changelog format, breaking-change conventions, release-notes pipeline
